description: A Logistics Operations Manager needs to track shipping performance based on 'business days' rather than calendar days in the AdventureWorks2022 database using Azure Data Studio. Implement a business day tracking system including a 'Sales.HolidayReference' table (populated with 2011-05-30, 2011-07-04, 2011-09-05, 2011-11-24, 2011-12-25, 2012-01-01, 2012-05-28, and 2012-07-04) and a scalar function 'dbo.fn_GetNetWorkingDays(@StartDate DATETIME, @EndDate DATETIME)' that returns an INT of working days (excluding weekends and holidays, exclusive of StartDate, inclusive of EndDate). Use these to create a view 'Sales.vw_ShippingSLABreach' identifying orders from 'Sales.SalesOrderHeader' where shipping took longer than 3 business days, featuring columns SalesOrderID, OrderDate, ShipDate, CalendarDaysTaken, and BusinessDaysTaken. Export the top 100 rows, ordered by BusinessDaysTaken descending, to /home/ga/Documents/sla_breaches.csv with headers.
